# Contextful

> Local-first, privacy-aware company brain. Humans and their AI agents collaborate in shared documents, and every agent sees only the context it is permitted to — scoped by capability. No company-wide "SuperAgent".

## Pages

- [Home](https://www.contextful.work/): what Contextful is, the demo video, the deck, and the spec
- [Demo](https://demo.contextful.work/): live collaborative demo

## Docs — how it works

Full corpus in one fetch: [llms-full.txt](https://www.contextful.work/llms-full.txt). Each page is also served as Markdown at the same path with `.md`.

- [Local-first & data ingestion](https://www.contextful.work/docs/local-first-ingestion/): on-prem host, connectors, acl-tagged events, Markdown memory ([md](https://www.contextful.work/docs/local-first-ingestion.md))
- [Sandbox & capability tokens](https://www.contextful.work/docs/sandbox-capability-tokens/): per-document sandboxes, attenuated Biscuit tokens, field/row redaction, access requests ([md](https://www.contextful.work/docs/sandbox-capability-tokens.md))
- [Collaboration & CRDT](https://www.contextful.work/docs/collaboration-crdt/): Loro CRDT rooms, on-prem relay, presence, agents as peers ([md](https://www.contextful.work/docs/collaboration-crdt.md))

## Highlights

- Capability-based access control with attenuation — an agent's access is provably a subset of its owner's (e.g. the CTO's agent cannot read the CEO's salary).
- A memory/brain that synthesizes context from your tools, detects anomalies, and grows over time — exposed over MCP.
- Local-first sync (Loro CRDT) over Tailscale; cloud is optional, used only for sync transport and LLM inference.
